ref: Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, “Why are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!”
text: Yesu gi dɛsɛ yɛɛ mɛ kerɛ ansi daa ne mɛ kya taasɛ mɔ. Imɔso ɔ taasɛ mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Menɛ so ne fɛ kya soo me idoo gɛnen? Ɔko ɔꞌ lɛɛ aterenbi gibi ba de nꞌ kerɛ.”
translated: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him. Then a man with a skin disease came and bowed before Jesus. He said, “Lord, you can heal me if you want.” Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Imɔso ɔnyen mɔ‑rɛ mɔ-ka mɛŋ baa mɛ gyɛ asa anyɔ, mɛ bingiri nyamesɛ kolon. Ibono Wurubuaarɛ ne n kyu mɔmɔ dabɔlɛ wɔra nyamesɛ kolon faa mɔ, ɔko ɔŋ sa ɔꞌ baragɛ mɔmɔ dɔ.”
translated: If you had not gone and brought the gun, the fetish would not have caught you. It is the Wurubuaare fetish that has caught you, and it will not let you go until you have died.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Nfono mɔ, ilaa nyɛnyɛn gɛmu mɔ, mɔ ɔbono ɔ kya soo anyamesɛ idoo mɔ, gi ba mɔ asɛ baa tɔgɛ mɔ yɛɛ, “Nengyene gɛsintin fo gyɛ Wurubuaarɛ mɔ-bi mɔ, tɔgɛ abui adɛ yɛɛ aꞌ bingiri agyudɔ.”
translated: My son, in this very hour, the Lord has revealed to me that you will be a great leader, and he said to me, "I have chosen you to lead my people, so that you may bring them out of bondage."

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.

translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith? How is it that you have no faith and do not trust in the power of my Father, who is in heaven?”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Wura Hɛrodɛ gi nu ilaa ibono so mɛ naa mɔ, i kyogedɛ mɔ-gisen dɔ. Gɛnen kee ne i kyogedɛ Gyɛrusalem awura mɔ pɛwu asen dɔ ne.
translated: King Herod heard about Jesus and was troubled, because people were saying he was John the Baptist risen from the dead. Others said he was Elijah or one of the prophets of old.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Amudɛnsɛbo ne n gyɛ asa abono mɔmɔ-asen dɔ i kya dɛɛ mɔmɔ mɔ. Wurubuaarɛ laa yɛgɛ mɔmɔ-asen aꞌ tɔrɔ mɔmɔ.
translated: The elders and I have been discussing the matter for a long time. The chief also agrees with the matter.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Mɔmɔ-ilaa kyosɛ i nyaakyɔ gikyɔ. Nengyene ba‑a mɛ lii gigya ba mɔ, mɛ kya kyu nkyu ŋmadɛ mɔmɔ-nyoro pɛi de mɛꞌ lɔɔ nyɛ gyi. Ne mɛ kya kyu nkyu ŋmadɛ nwɛ, nlɔbi, de ikuruwa de sɛi ɔŋꞌ sa ɔꞌ kyɔlesɛ mɔmɔ-Wurubuaarɛ ɔson.
translated: I am writing this in Gikyode. If you are reading this and you understand it, then you are truly one of our people. If you are reading this and you don't understand, may God, the creator, protect you on behalf of the Gikyode people.

text: Me so mɔ, ɔkamaasɛ laa kyo fɛye. Ɔbono ɔ laa yelɛ mɔ-ayaa so ka gyan me so kpaa lii mɔ, Wurubuaarɛ laa mɔlɛgɛ mɔ de ɔꞌ nyɛ ɔkyenaten mɔ asɛ. Nkɛ Nbono Dɔ-Lɛɛ Awɔrɔfɔɔ Mɔ A Laa Don Awɔrɔfɔɔ Kamaasɛ

translated: I want to tell a story about a certain rich man. He had many wives and children. Among the children, there was one particular child who, when he grew up, asked his father, "Father, what is the one thing you own that if I ask for it, you will give it to me without hesitation?"

text: Gɛsintin, me-sɛ. Imɔ ibono ne n wɔra fo ɔbolɛ.”

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
text: tɔgɛ yɛɛ, “Ɔnyen baarɛ gi tɔgɛ yɛɛ ɔ laa taalɛ boori Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ de oꞌ kyu nkɛ nsa kii kyu yii mɔ.”
translated: Then he said, “The reason I am here is that I have come to ask Wurubua to give me a charm so that I can get money.”

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ɔko gi nyise fo yɛɛ foꞌ sola mɔ-gɛsola sa mɔ gikpaaten kolon mɔ, sola sa mɔ de fo‑rɛ mɔ fɛꞌ naa akpaaten anyɔ.
translated: If you do not listen to the word of God, you will not be saved, and you will not enter the kingdom of God.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Imɔ‑rɛ imɔ gɛnen mɔ, aŋ kya laarɛ aꞌ wɔra too-too asɔɔbo mɔ ginyadon. Imɔso kpe ɔbon mɔ dɔ kpaa too daaruwa. Ɔken ɔbono fo laa daa gilɛɛ mɔ, kerɛ mɔ-gɛnɔ dɔ, fo laa wu aterenbi gibi. Lɛɛ gimɔ de foꞌ kyu kpaa too me‑rɛ fo-lɛɛ.”
translated: If you want to eat a toad, you must look for a fat and beautiful one. And when you have killed it, you must not put it on the ground. Because if you put it on the ground, it will become soiled and you will not enjoy eating it. So you must hold it in your hand until you have finished eating it.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Imɔ gɛmara mɔ, Yesu gi kii kpe Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ gikpaara mɔ so ɔ kya nyiile asa ilaa, ne Gyuda awura asunbi alɛɛbo abelɛnsɛ mɔ de mɔmɔ abelɛnsɛ sɛnsɛ ako mɛ ba mɔ asɛ baa taasɛ mɔ yɛɛ, “Ilaa ibonoana fo kya wɔra faa mɔ, anɛ ginyen dɔ ne fo kya wɔra imɔ daa? Anɛ ne n sa fo ɔkpa yɛɛ foꞌ wɔra imɔ?”
translated: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he said to his disciples, “As you know, the Passover is two days away—and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.” Then the chief priests and the elders of the people assembled in the palace of the high priest, whose name was Caiaphas, and they schemed to arrest Jesus secretly and kill him. “But not during the festival,” they said, “or there may be a riot among the people.”

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Fɛye berɛ, fɛꞌ laarɛ daa gɛnɔɔbono Wurubuaarɛ gɛwuragyi dɔ akyenabi a laa ba asa dɔ mɔ de fɛꞌ nyanga ansi gyi gɛsintin sa Wurubuaarɛ. Fɛ ti fɛ kya wɔra gɛnen mɔ, Wurubuaarɛ laa yɛgɛ ilaa sɛnsɛ idɛana pɛwu kee iꞌ yii fɛye-abaa.
translated: Long ago, the people of Laare were always defeating Wurubuaare in war until one day they decided to go and consult a soothsayer to find out the secret behind Wurubuaare's strength. When they went, the Wurubuaare chief asked them to bring a white chicken and an egg.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Ako kee a tɔrɔ iwe dɔ. Abono a kɔrɔ mɔ, iwe mɔ kee i kɔrɔ koso gɛɛlɛ amɔ, ne a mɛŋ nyɛ da amu de aꞌ wɔra abi.
translated: I have a book. If you want it, I will give it to you, but you must return it to me when you are done reading.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Yesu mɔ‑rɛ mɔmɔ mɛ naa kpaa loo Kapɛɛniyon. Mɛ bo nno mɔ, mɔmɔ-gɛkɛ kyoolasɛ gɛ fo. Asa mɛ kpaa gyan mɔmɔ-gikyangbon dɔ, ne Yesu gi nyiile mɔmɔ Wurubuaarɛ ilaa nno.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "You will all fall away because of me this night. For it is written, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.' But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee."

ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
text: ne ɔ tɔgɛ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Nɛ fɛ ɔnyen ɔbono ɔŋ wɔra ilaa nyɛnyɛn mɔ sa fɛye kaaborɛ ɔ kya laarɛ ɔꞌ nyɛ lɛwu faa, nɛ wɔra ilaa nyɛnyɛn!” Mɛ lɛɛ gɛnɔ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Imɔ ibono iŋ fɛ aye-giso. Fo-nkon ilaa ne!”
translated: Then the tortoise said, "If you want to kill me, throw me into the water and let me die there, throw me into the water!" And the leopard said, "You are a very cunning person. Take him away!"

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Yesu mɛŋ baa bugi mɔ-gɛnɔ tɔgɛ sɛi, imɔso i tansi dɛ ɔbelɛnsɛ mɔ gɛnɔ. Pilato Gi Sa Ɔkpa Yɛɛ Mɛꞌ Mɔɔ Yesu
translated: When Jesus was brought before the governor, he asked him, "Are you the king of the Jews?" Jesus answered, "You have said so." Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, "I find no guilt in this man."
